MONETTE MANOR, LLC

Address
669 HWY 139 NORTH, MONETTE, AR 72447
Phone

Avg daily residents

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.59 3.47 2.87 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.27 0.61 0.30 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.51 0.77 0.57 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.81 2.10 2.01 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 0.78 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.28
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.11
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.53
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.23
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.89
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-09-26 12 11 1 76 1 76
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-09-28 0 0 0 0 0 0
Total weighted health score
57.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (12)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

12 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-12-31 2026-01-30
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. E 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0837 Establish a governing body that is legally responsible for establishing and implementing policies for managing and operating the facility and appoints a properly licensed administrator responsible for managing the facility. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0839 Employ staff that are licensed, certified, or registered in accordance with state laws. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-09-26 2024-10-26
